_meta.description: SageReasoning Compliance Register — maps external regulatory obligations to mandate rules

obligations.notes: EU AI Office published Art. 6 classification guidelines in Feb 2026. EU Digital Omnibus proposes postponing high-risk obligations by up to 16 months (backstop Dec 2027 for Annex III). SageReasoning evaluates reasoning quality, not persons — profiling exemption likely applies. FOUNDER ACTION REQUIRED: Review Art. 6 guidelines to confirm SageReasoning's agent trust layer is not classified as high-risk under Annex III (particularly biometric/profiling categories).
actions: PRIORITY: Review Feb 2026 Art. 6 classification guidelines against agent trust layer
Monitor Digital Omnibus legislative progress — if adopted before Aug 2026, high-risk deadline extends
Assess whether agent trust layer constitutes profiling under Art. 6
Review R3 disclaimer adequacy against Art. 50 Code of Practice v2 (March 2026)

obligations.notes: Second Draft Code of Practice for Art. 50 (marking/labelling AI-generated content) published March 2026. R3 disclaimer satisfies Art. 50 transparency. API responses identify AI-generated content. No action required at this time — confirm alignment at next review when final Code of Practice is published.
actions: Confirm R3 disclaimer text meets final Art. 50 Code of Practice when published
Monitor whether watermarking/labelling requirements extend to text-based evaluation outputs

obligations.obligation: EU AI Act — GPAI Model Obligations
obligations.notes: SageReasoning uses third-party GPAI (Claude); does not train or distribute its own foundation model. Digital Omnibus may clarify downstream deployer obligations. Monitor.
actions: Monitor for downstream deployer obligation guidance from EU AI Office
Monitor Digital Omnibus for deployer-specific GPAI obligations

obligations.notes: VAISS remains voluntary. Dec 2025 National AI Plan confirmed no mandatory conversion at this time. Australian AI Safety Institute (AISI) becoming operational in early 2026 — monitoring for enforcement signals. No status change required.
actions: Monitor AISI publications once operational for enforcement signals
Track outcome of mandatory guardrails consultation for high-risk settings
Review VAISS × Guidance for AI Adoption crosswalk alignment

obligations.external_reference: Privacy Act 1988, APPs 1–13; Privacy and Other Legislation Amendment Act 2024
obligations.notes: MAJOR CHANGE: Privacy and Other Legislation Amendment Act 2024 passed Dec 2024. Automated decision-making transparency obligations take effect 10 Dec 2026. Businesses using AI to make decisions that could significantly affect individuals must disclose this in privacy policies. Children's Online Privacy Code also due by Dec 2026. FOUNDER ACTION REQUIRED: Privacy policy must be updated before Dec 2026 to comply with APP 1.7 automated decision-making transparency. Age verification for under-16 users needs review against Children's Online Privacy Code.
actions: PRIORITY: Update privacy policy for APP 1.7 automated decision-making transparency before Dec 2026
Review Children's Online Privacy Code requirements when OAIC publishes draft
Assess whether age verification gate (currently under-16 parental consent) meets Code requirements
Implement /api/user/export and /api/user/delete endpoints (existing deferred item)
Obtain lawyer review of privacy policy before Dec 2026 deadline

obligations.external_reference: Competition and Consumer Act 2010, Sch 2, Part 2-1
obligations.notes: ACCC 2026-27 enforcement priorities include AI-related digital market conduct and ghost websites using generative AI. No direct enforcement actions against AI evaluation services found. R3 disclaimer continues to prevent misleading conduct (s18) and R9 prevents false representations (s29). No status change required.
actions: Monitor ACCC enforcement actions against AI evaluation services
Review marketing materials against s18/s29 at each review cycle

obligations.notes: No updates to the standard itself in Q1 2026. New implementation guide published Feb 2026 — useful for gap analysis when pursuing certification. Manifest rules map to several Annex A controls. Full certification not pursued at launch.
actions: Map R0–R20 to specific Annex A control objectives using Feb 2026 implementation guide
Identify gaps between current controls and ISO 42001 requirements
Reassess certification value when revenue exceeds $50K/year

obligations.notes: NIST Cyber AI Profile under development (comment period closed, initial public draft expected 2026). AI RMF 1.1 addenda and expanded profiles expected through 2026. No material change to RMF 1.0 core. No status change required.
actions: Monitor NIST Cyber AI Profile publication
Adopt NIST AI RMF vocabulary in compliance documentation
Review AI RMF 1.1 addenda when published

obligations.external_reference: Regulation 2016/679, Art. 5, 6, 9, 22, 35; proposed Digital Omnibus amendments
obligations.notes: EU Digital Omnibus proposes legitimate interest basis for AI development/operation and new exemption for residual special-category data. If adopted, this may simplify GDPR compliance for SageReasoning's AI processing. GDPR delete/export endpoints remain a deferred item (Security Audit D8). No EU marketing at launch. Status unchanged — implement before EU expansion.
actions: Monitor Digital Omnibus GDPR amendments progress through EU Parliament
Implement GDPR delete/export endpoints (Security Audit D8)
Conduct Data Protection Impact Assessment before EU marketing
Assess Art. 22 applicability to agent evaluation outputs
Evaluate whether legitimate interest basis (if adopted) applies to sage-reason processing

obligations.notes: ASIC Moneysmart published AI financial guidance March 2026 — warns consumers about trusting AI for financial decisions. ASIC emphasises existing AFSL obligations apply fully to AI-powered tools. Reinforces need for lawyer review before sage-invest marketplace listing. No status change.
actions: Obtain Australian financial services lawyer opinion on sage-invest
Do not list sage-invest on marketplace until lawyer review complete
Monitor ASIC guidance on AI-generated financial product advice
Review sage-invest disclaimers against ASIC Moneysmart guidance

obligations.notes: Business plan allocates ~$1,200/yr. Not yet purchased. Must be in place before public launch.
actions: Obtain professional indemnity insurance quotes
Ensure policy covers AI-generated philosophical evaluation errors
Purchase before public launch

obligations.notes: Business plan allocates ~$600/yr. Not yet purchased. Must be in place before public launch.
actions: Obtain cyber insurance quotes
Ensure policy covers Supabase-hosted data and API breach scenarios
Purchase before public launch

obligations.notes: Patent pending on philosophical evaluation framework architecture. File before public API launch.
actions: Prepare patent application for philosophical evaluation framework
File Q2 2026
Monitor competitor 'Stoic AI' claims quarterly

obligations.external_reference: Terms of Service, new Embedding section
obligations.notes: R13 created. ToS embedding section drafted in Policy Amendments doc. Not yet implemented in code.
actions: Implement embedding platform ToS section in website/src/app/terms/page.tsx
Add R13 compliance check to API onboarding flow
Create embedding platform agreement template

obligations.notes: Compliance matrix created (6A in Marketplace Compliance Amendment). 9/16 skills pass. 3 fail, 4 edge cases documented.
actions: Run compliance matrix for each new skill before marketplace listing
Resolve 4 edge case skills (sage-invest, sage-compliance, sage-negotiate, sage-coach)
Implement automated R10/R12 checks in skill submission pipeline

obligations.notes: Session bridge module (session-bridge.ts) captures strategic decisions from Claude Cowork sessions and routes them through the Sage Mentor ring. Three modes: observer, consultant, companion. Classification gate ensures zero LLM cost for routine exchanges. R12 minimum-2-mechanism enforcement on every evaluation.
actions: Integration test: verify R12 minimum 2 mechanisms per evaluation
Integration test: verify R3 disclaimer present on all evaluative output
Validate classification gate accuracy against 50 sample exchanges
Confirm R1 no-therapeutic-implication language in all prompt builders

obligations.notes: Two new Supabase tables (session_decisions, session_context_snapshots) store Cowork session evaluation results. RLS enabled on both tables. Migration SQL created at supabase/migrations/20260404_session_bridge_tables.sql. R4 IP protection via RLS ensures users access only their own records.
actions: Run migration against Supabase staging environment
Verify RLS policies block cross-user access
Test cascade delete from session_decisions to session_context_snapshots
Add session_decisions to GDPR delete/export endpoints (CR-009 D8)

obligations.obligation: Session Bridge — Live Companion Mode Cost Controls
obligations.notes: Companion mode runs Haiku calls per classified exchange (~$0.01–$0.04/session). Classification gate filters 60–80% of exchanges as routine/informational (zero cost). Progressive depth routes complex evaluations to Sonnet only when concerns are detected. Token budget cap prevents runaway costs.
actions: Implement token budget cap per session (configurable, default in SessionBridgeConfig)
Monitor actual cost per session during beta
Alert if companion mode cost exceeds $0.10 per session
Verify classification gate filters routine exchanges correctly

obligations.notes: Sage-consult Cowork skill allows founder to invoke mentor consultation mid-session. Skill file documents governance rules enforced (R1, R3, R6c, R6d, R9, R12). Output format includes mandatory R3 disclaimer. R10 marketplace certification required if skill is ever published externally.
actions: Test skill invocation end-to-end in Cowork session
Verify R3 disclaimer appears in all output paths
Run R10/R12 compliance matrix before any external listing
Document skill in Support Agent Manual §8 (Session Bridge)

obligations.notes: Pattern engine extended to detect longitudinal patterns across Cowork sessions: decision domain clustering, passion recurrence in strategic contexts, proximity trajectory per inner agent, value-action consistency, and strategic blind spots. Weekly strategic mirror (Sonnet) synthesises cross-session patterns. R6c qualitative levels only. R6d diagnostic not punitive. R7 source fidelity maintained via ring prompt builders.
actions: Validate pattern detectors produce qualitative (not numeric) outputs
Test weekly strategic mirror prompt for R6d non-punitive language
Verify R7 source fidelity — all journal references traceable to original entries
Monitor false positive rate on strategic blind spot detection

obligations.obligation: Intimate Data Protection — Passion Profiling Safeguards
obligations.notes: R17 requires protections beyond standard database security for journal interpretation data (passion maps, trigger maps, contradiction maps, developmental timelines). The ethical analysis identified that this data is qualitatively different from identity or financial data — a breach enables psychological manipulation. Five sub-requirements: (a) bulk profiling prevention via technical measures, (b) application-level encryption for intimate fields, (c) defined retention periods with genuine deletion, (d) local-first storage consideration for highest-sensitivity data, (e) passion profiling results never exposed via API.
actions: PRIORITY: Implement API-level rate limiting that distinguishes self-evaluation from third-party evaluation (R17a)
Design application-level encryption for passion_map, trigger_map, contradiction_map fields in Supabase (R17b)
Define and document retention periods for all profile data categories (R17c)
Implement genuine deletion (not soft-delete) for intimate profile fields via /api/user/delete (R17c)
Evaluate local-first architecture for trigger maps and contradiction maps — produce architecture decision record (R17d)
Audit all API endpoints to confirm passion profiling results are never returned (R17e)
Update privacy policy to describe intimate data protections before launch (links to CR-005)

obligations.obligation: Honest Certification — Agent Trust Layer Transparency
obligations.notes: R18 addresses the certifier problem: SageReasoning positions itself as a certification authority, which concentrates power and creates obligations of honesty. Four sub-requirements: (a) certification scope language must state it certifies 'observable reasoning patterns as measured against the Stoic philosophical framework', (b) trust badge must link to methodology documentation, (c) architecture must support interoperability with other ethical reasoning traditions, (d) adversarial evaluation must be conducted before broad deployment.
actions: PRIORITY: Draft certification scope language for trust badge, accreditation card, and all public-facing documentation (R18a)
Create methodology documentation page that badge links to — covering evaluation process, limitations, what it does not certify (R18b)
Review Agent Trust Layer schema for interoperability hooks — ensure accreditation schema can accommodate non-Stoic certification providers (R18c)
Design adversarial evaluation protocol — identify gaming strategies for the 4-stage evaluation sequence (R18d)
Conduct adversarial testing before any external agent accreditation is issued (R18d)
Update agent-card.json and llms.txt with honest certification scope language (R18a)

obligations.obligation: Honest Positioning — Cultural and Philosophical Transparency
obligations.notes: R19 addresses the reasoning monoculture and cultural imperialism risks. SageReasoning encodes one philosophical tradition among many. Four sub-requirements: (a) cultural honesty — all content must acknowledge Stoic origin, (b) no universality claims — avoid positioning as 'the' standard, (c) limitations documented and accessible, (d) mirror principle — framework is for self-examination not judging others. The ethical analysis identified that honest positioning is itself a Stoic virtue — the framework's integrity depends on not overclaiming.
actions: PRIORITY: Audit all user-facing website content for universality claims — revise to honest positioning (R19a, R19b)
Audit API documentation and developer docs for positioning language (R19a, R19b)
Create limitations documentation page — covering Stoic tradition's blind spots (systemic critique, collective action, structural injustice) (R19c)
Implement mirror principle guidance in mentor persona prompts and all human-facing tools (R19d)
Review marketing materials, pitch deck, and growth strategy for language that positions SageReasoning as universal (R19b)
Update project instructions to replace 'the world's leading reference' with honest positioning language

obligations.obligation: Active Protection — Vulnerable Users, Independence, Human Override
obligations.notes: R20 addresses four active protection requirements that go beyond passive disclaimers. (a) Vulnerable user detection — active detection of acute psychological distress with redirection to professional support. (b) Independence not dependence — system designed to encourage internalisation, with dependence-pattern detection. (c) Human override supremacy — no accreditation level may make it harder for a human to override an agent. (d) Relationship asymmetry awareness — guidance that the framework is for self-examination, discouraging interpersonal application of passion taxonomy.
actions: DONE (17 Apr 2026): Two-stage classifier (regex + Haiku) wired on all 9 API routes. Client-side distress redirect UI on all 5 pages. Inline detection, not async queue per original ADR — interim implementation verified working. ADR-R20a-01 async queue design to be reconciled or superseded post-hold-point (R20a)
DONE (17 Apr 2026): Redirect UI shows crisis resources (988 Lifeline, Crisis Text Line, IASP). Persistent footer still pending build (R20a)
Design dependence-detection heuristics for usage patterns (e.g., trivial decisions evaluated, inability to reason without framework) (R20b)
Implement mentor response for dependence detection: 'You're ready to reason through this yourself' (R20b)
Review authority-manager.ts to confirm no authority level prevents human override — add explicit check if needed (R20c)
Add relationship asymmetry guidance to all human-facing tool output and mentor persona (R20d)
Update persona.ts morning/evening prompts to include mirror principle language (R20d)
Test vulnerable user detection against sample inputs before launch
